_readme: Demo Schema Isolation — full implementation plan. Two phases: Phase 1 (tasks 1–5) delivers a working isolated demo experience. Phase 2 (tasks 6–8) cleans up public schema contamination and optimises the production bundle. Tasks within each phase follow the dependency order listed.

phase_1.label: Core isolation — demo schema live, services routed, demo users see demo data
phase_1.outcome: Demo users are fully isolated in demo.* schema. Real users see zero Mock_ data. JS mock arrays still work for local dev.

phase_2.label: Cleanup and hardening — remove public contamination, optimise bundle
phase_2.outcome: Mock_ rows removed from public schema and seed file. Realtime works for demo users. Mock data excluded from production bundle.

objective: Create a separate `demo` Postgres schema in Supabase with mirrored tables for all entities that demo users interact with. Demo rows are completely isolated from the `public` schema — no real user can ever see them.
inputs: supabase/migrations/20260318_full_domain_schema.sql — table shapes to mirror
seed/01_seed_data.sql — current Mock_ rows to move into demo schema
seed/00_seed_auth_users.sql — demo user UUIDs (00000000-0000-0000-0000-00000000000x pattern)
constraints: Demo schema tables must exactly mirror public schema column shapes — no divergence
RLS on demo schema: only rows owned by demo user UUIDs are accessible; real user UUIDs never match
demo_expires_at column added to every demo table — NULL means permanent seed row
Do NOT alter any public schema table — zero coupling
Migration must be idempotent (CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS, CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
deliverables: supabase/migrations/20260408100000_demo_schema_create.sql — creates demo schema + mirrors tables: patients, caregiver_profiles, guardian_profiles, agencies, placements, shifts, care_contracts, care_contract_bids, invoices, payment_proofs, wallets, wallet_transactions, jobs
Each table gets a demo_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ column (nullable — NULL = permanent)
RLS policies on each demo table: SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE allowed only for demo user UUIDs
Helper function: demo.is_demo_user() → bool — checks if auth.uid() is a known demo UUID
definition_of_done: SELECT * FROM demo.patients returns rows; SELECT * FROM public.patients as a real user returns nothing from demo
A real authenticated user querying demo.patients gets 0 rows (RLS blocks)
A demo user querying demo.patients gets their seeded rows
demo_expires_at column exists on all demo tables
validation_steps: Run migration in Supabase SQL editor — no errors
As real user JWT: SELECT * FROM demo.care_contracts → 0 rows
As demo user JWT: SELECT * FROM demo.care_contracts → seeded rows visible
Describe demo.patients → demo_expires_at column present
failure_conditions: Real user can see any demo schema row
Missing demo_expires_at column on any table
Any foreign key reference from demo schema into public schema (no cross-schema FKs)
Migration fails on re-run (not idempotent)

objective: Insert all permanent Mock_ seed rows into the demo schema tables. These rows have demo_expires_at = NULL and are never auto-deleted. This is the baseline demo experience every demo user sees.
inputs: seed/01_seed_data.sql — all existing Mock_ rows (patients, caregiver_profiles, guardian_profiles, agencies, placements, shifts, care_contracts, care_contract_bids, invoices, wallets, etc.)
seed/00_seed_auth_users.sql — demo user UUIDs for owner/guardian_id columns
constraints: demo_expires_at = NULL on every row (permanent, never auto-deleted)
All UUIDs match the existing demo user UUIDs (00000000-0000-0000-0000-00000000000x)
Use ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE to make this re-runnable without duplicates
Must NOT insert these rows into public schema tables — public seed file to be cleaned up separately
deliverables: supabase/migrations/20260408110000_demo_schema_seed.sql — inserts all Mock_ rows into demo.* tables with demo_expires_at = NULL

Covers: demo.patients, demo.caregiver_profiles, demo.guardian_profiles, demo.agencies, demo.placements, demo.shifts, demo.care_contracts, demo.care_contract_bids, demo.wallets, demo.wallet_transactions, demo.jobs
definition_of_done: After running migration: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM demo.care_contracts → matches current 01_seed_data.sql contract count
All demo_expires_at values are NULL
Migration re-runnable without errors or duplicate rows
validation_steps: Run seed migration in SQL editor
SELECT id, title, demo_expires_at FROM demo.care_contracts → rows visible, expires_at is NULL
Run migration again — no unique constraint violations
As demo user in browser: guardian dashboard shows Mock_ patients, care contracts
failure_conditions: Any demo_expires_at set to a non-NULL value on permanent seed rows
Rows inserted into public.* instead of demo.*
Migration fails on second run (not idempotent)
Missing rows for any entity type the demo UI expects

objective: Schedule a pg_cron job that runs on the first day of every month and deletes all demo schema rows where demo_expires_at < NOW(). Permanent seed rows (demo_expires_at = NULL) are never touched.
inputs: Supabase project with pg_cron extension enabled
demo schema with demo_expires_at column on all tables

List of all demo tables to clean: patients, caregiver_profiles, guardian_profiles, agencies, placements, shifts, care_contracts, care_contract_bids, invoices, payment_proofs, wallets, wallet_transactions, jobs
constraints: Only delete rows WHERE demo_expires_at IS NOT NULL AND demo_expires_at < NOW()
Never delete rows where demo_expires_at IS NULL (permanent seeds)
Cron schedule: '0 0 1 * *' — midnight UTC on the 1st of every month
Idempotent: running cleanup manually between cron ticks must be safe
Log a count of deleted rows per table to pg_cron.job_run_details
deliverables: supabase/migrations/20260408120000_demo_cleanup_cron.sql — creates pg_cron job 'demo-monthly-cleanup'
Cleanup function: demo.cleanup_expired_rows() — iterates all demo tables and deletes expired rows
Cron registration: SELECT cron.schedule('demo-monthly-cleanup', '0 0 1 * *', 'SELECT demo.cleanup_expired_rows()')
definition_of_done: SELECT * FROM cron.job WHERE jobname = 'demo-monthly-cleanup' → 1 row
INSERT a test row with demo_expires_at = NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day' → run cleanup manually → row deleted
INSERT a row with demo_expires_at = NULL → run cleanup → row still exists
INSERT a row with demo_expires_at = NOW() + INTERVAL '10 days' → run cleanup → row still exists (not yet expired)
validation_steps: Run migration — no errors
SELECT * FROM cron.job WHERE jobname = 'demo-monthly-cleanup' → schedule is '0 0 1 * *'
INSERT INTO demo.care_contracts (..., demo_expires_at = NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day')
SELECT demo.cleanup_expired_rows() → returns deleted counts
Verify the past-expired row is gone; permanent rows (NULL) untouched
failure_conditions: Cleanup deletes rows with demo_expires_at = NULL (permanent seeds destroyed)
Cron job not registered (SELECT from cron.job returns nothing)
pg_cron extension not enabled — migration fails with 'extension not found'
Cleanup function errors silently and returns without deleting anything
notes: If pg_cron is not available in your Supabase plan tier, an alternative is a Supabase Edge Function triggered by an external cron service (e.g. GitHub Actions scheduled workflow calling POST /cleanup-demo). The same SQL logic applies.

objective: Add a centralised isDemoSession() check in the frontend service layer so every service routes demo users to demo.* tables and real users to public.* tables. No service should have ad-hoc demo detection logic.
inputs: src/backend/services/_sb.ts — shared Supabase query helpers
src/backend/services/guardian.service.ts — existing isDemoAuthMode() pattern to generalise
src/backend/services/supabase.ts — USE_SUPABASE flag
src/frontend/auth/mockAuth.ts — demo email/id conventions (@carenet.demo, demo- prefix)
constraints: isDemoSession() must work without async calls — read from localStorage only (same approach as isDemoAuthMode())
sbDemo() helper must return sb().schema('demo') — Supabase JS client supports .schema() natively
shouldUseSupabase() must stay: true only when USE_SUPABASE=true AND not a demo session
All existing services that currently have ad-hoc demo checks must be migrated to use these shared helpers
E2E tests using VITE_PLAYWRIGHT_E2E=true must still go through mock mode (no demo schema hit)
deliverables: src/backend/services/_sb.ts — add isDemoSession(), sbDemo(), shouldUseSupabase() (already exists in guardian.service — move here), demoDelay() helper
src/backend/services/guardian.service.ts — remove local isDemoAuthMode(), import from _sb
Document the three-mode decision tree as a comment block in _sb.ts: (1) E2E/no-env → mock arrays, (2) USE_SUPABASE + demo session → demo schema, (3) USE_SUPABASE + real session → public schema
definition_of_done: isDemoSession() returns true for @carenet.demo emails and demo- id prefixes
isDemoSession() returns false for any real user
sbDemo().from('care_contracts').select('*') queries demo schema, not public
All services use shouldUseSupabase() not raw USE_SUPABASE for read/write decisions
guardian.service.ts no longer has its own isDemoAuthMode() — uses _sb import
validation_steps: Log in as guardian@carenet.demo → isDemoSession() === true in browser console
Log in as a real Supabase user → isDemoSession() === false
Check network tab: demo user's patient fetch hits schema=demo query parameter
Check network tab: real user's patient fetch hits no schema parameter (defaults to public)
failure_conditions: isDemoSession() throws or returns wrong value for edge-case emails
Real user accidentally routed to demo schema
Demo user accidentally routed to public schema (sees real data or empty results)
E2E tests hitting demo schema instead of mock arrays

objective: Update all backend services to route demo users to demo.* tables instead of returning static JS mock arrays. Real users continue to use public schema. USE_SUPABASE=false (no env vars) still falls back to JS mock arrays for local dev.
inputs: src/backend/services/_sb.ts (with isDemoSession, sbDemo, shouldUseSupabase)

All services: guardian.service.ts, marketplace.service.ts, billing.service.ts, caregiver.service.ts, agency.service.ts, patient.service.ts, schedule.service.ts, message.service.ts, notification.service.ts, support.service.ts, admin.service.ts, community.service.ts, shop.service.ts, section15.service.ts, moderator.service.ts, contractService.ts, walletService.ts
src/backend/api/mock/* — JS mock arrays (remain for USE_SUPABASE=false fallback only)
constraints: Three-branch pattern per function: (1) !USE_SUPABASE → mock array, (2) isDemoSession() → sbDemo().from(...), (3) else → sb().from(...)
Demo INSERT/UPDATE must tag new rows: demo_expires_at = NOW() + INTERVAL '30 days'
Demo DELETE must only delete rows the demo user owns (owner_id or guardian_id matches demo UUID)
Do not remove JS mock arrays — they are the fallback for USE_SUPABASE=false (local dev, E2E)
deliverables: All ~17 service files updated with three-branch routing
Every demo INSERT includes demo_expires_at = NOW() + INTERVAL '30 days'
JS mock arrays retained but only reached when USE_SUPABASE is false
definition_of_done: Demo user logs in → patients list shows Mock_ rows from demo.patients (not JS array)
Demo user creates a new care request → row appears in demo.care_contracts with demo_expires_at ~30 days out
Real user logs in → public.patients shows only their own rows, zero Mock_ rows visible
Local dev with no VITE_SUPABASE_URL → JS mock arrays returned as before
validation_steps: Login as guardian@carenet.demo → open patients page → rows say Mock_Amina Begum etc.
In Supabase SQL editor: SELECT * FROM demo.patients → same rows visible
Create a new patient as demo user → SELECT from demo.patients → new row present with demo_expires_at NOT NULL
Login as real Supabase guardian → patients page shows only their own rows
In Supabase SQL editor: SELECT * FROM public.patients → zero Mock_ rows present
failure_conditions: Demo user sees empty lists (routed to public schema with mismatched UUIDs)
Real user sees Mock_ prefixed rows
Demo INSERT missing demo_expires_at (row becomes permanent accidentally)
Service still importing and returning from JS mock array when USE_SUPABASE=true

objective: Remove all Mock_ seed rows from the public schema (01_seed_data.sql and live DB) now that demo data lives exclusively in the demo schema. Prevents any chance of real users seeing demo rows.
inputs: seed/01_seed_data.sql — all Mock_ rows to remove
seed/00_seed_auth_users.sql — demo auth users to assess (keep for demo login; they live in auth.users not public schema)
Live Supabase DB — existing Mock_ rows in public.patients, public.care_contracts, etc.
constraints: Do NOT delete auth.users rows for demo accounts — demo users must still be able to log in via Supabase Auth
Only delete rows from public.* tables (not auth.users)
Create a migration rather than running ad-hoc SQL so the cleanup is tracked
Update seed/01_seed_data.sql to remove all Mock_ INSERT statements (they now live in demo schema seed migration)
deliverables: supabase/migrations/20260408130000_remove_public_mock_rows.sql — DELETE FROM public.patients WHERE id IN (...demo UUIDs...) etc. for all affected tables
seed/01_seed_data.sql updated: Mock_ INSERT blocks removed (file kept for any future real seed data)
seed/README.md updated: note that demo data lives in demo schema migration, not here
definition_of_done: SELECT * FROM public.patients WHERE name LIKE 'Mock_%' → 0 rows
SELECT * FROM public.care_contracts WHERE title LIKE 'Mock_%' → 0 rows
Demo users can still log in with guardian@carenet.demo + demo1234
Demo users see their data from demo schema (not affected by this cleanup)
validation_steps: Run migration in SQL editor
SELECT name FROM public.patients → no Mock_ names
SELECT title FROM public.care_contracts → no Mock_ titles
Log in as guardian@carenet.demo → data still visible (from demo schema, not public)
Log in as real guardian → no Mock_ data visible anywhere
failure_conditions: Demo auth users deleted from auth.users — demo login breaks
Migration deletes rows from demo schema instead of public schema
Real user data accidentally deleted (must only target known demo UUIDs)
seed/01_seed_data.sql still contains Mock_ INSERTs after cleanup

objective: Update Supabase Realtime subscriptions in realtime.ts to listen on the demo schema when the session is a demo session, so demo users receive realtime events from demo.* tables, not public.*.
inputs: src/backend/services/realtime.ts — subscribeToMonetization, subscribeToAllMonetization, subscribeToCareContractBids, subscribeToBillingForUser
src/backend/services/_sb.ts — isDemoSession()
supabase/migrations/20260408100000_demo_schema_create.sql — demo schema tables added to realtime publication
constraints: Supabase Realtime postgres_changes requires tables to be in supabase_realtime publication — demo schema tables must be added
Channel names should include a 'demo:' prefix when in demo mode to avoid collisions with real user channels
isDemoSession() check must happen at subscription time, not at module load time
Unsubscribe/cleanup logic is unchanged
deliverables: realtime.ts — each subscription function checks isDemoSession() and sets schema: 'demo' on postgres_changes listener when true
supabase/migrations/20260408100000_demo_schema_create.sql updated to ADD demo schema tables to supabase_realtime publication
Channel names prefixed: 'demo:monetization:{userId}' vs 'monetization:{userId}'
definition_of_done: Demo user creates a bid in demo schema → realtime event fires → UI updates without refresh
Real user's realtime channel does not receive demo schema events
Channel name in Supabase dashboard shows 'demo:' prefix for demo sessions
validation_steps: Log in as demo guardian → open marketplace hub → submit a bid
Observe UI updates (toast or list refresh) without page reload
Check Supabase Realtime inspector → channel name has 'demo:' prefix
Log in as real user → no demo schema events received
failure_conditions: Demo schema tables not in supabase_realtime publication — silent channel failure
Real user channel accidentally subscribed to demo schema
Channel name collision between demo and real user sessions

objective: Convert JS mock array imports in all services from static top-level imports to dynamic imports so Vite excludes the entire mock/ folder from the production bundle when USE_SUPABASE=true.
inputs: src/backend/api/mock/*.ts — all mock data files
All service files that import from @/backend/api/mock
vite.config.ts — to verify tree-shaking / code-splitting behavior
constraints: Dynamic import only called in the !USE_SUPABASE branch — never reached in production
USE_SUPABASE is a build-time constant (import.meta.env) so Vite can statically eliminate the dead branch
Must not break local dev (USE_SUPABASE=false path must still work)
Do not restructure mock files — only change how they are imported
deliverables: Each service: replace `import { MOCK_X } from '@/backend/api/mock'` with `const { MOCK_X } = await import('@/backend/api/mock/xMocks')` inside the !USE_SUPABASE branch
Verify with `npx vite build --mode production` that dist/ does not contain mock data strings (e.g. 'Mock_Amina Begum')
definition_of_done: Production build (VITE_SUPABASE_URL set): grep dist/ for 'Mock_' → 0 matches
Local dev build (no VITE_SUPABASE_URL): mock arrays still load and return data correctly
Bundle size reduction visible in vite build output (mock files no longer in chunk)
validation_steps: npm run build with VITE_SUPABASE_URL set
grep -r 'Mock_Amina' dist/ → no results
npm run dev with no VITE_SUPABASE_URL → guardian page shows JS mock patients
Check vite build chunk report — no mock*.ts files in output chunks
failure_conditions: Mock data strings present in production bundle (tree-shaking failed)
Local dev breaks — mock arrays unreachable after dynamic import change
Dynamic import causes async waterfall in the critical render path
